cv_text:
Muhammad Bilal
methebilalashiq@gmail.com | +923088660209 | bilal-ashiq | thecallmeBilalAshiq | Faisalabad
OBJECTIVE:
Final-year Computer Science student at FAST NUCES with hands-on experience in software development,
AI/ML, and full-stack technologies. Seeking Software Developer or AI Engineer roles to build intelligent,
scalable solutions and drive real-world impact.
EDUCATION
BS (Computer Science)-CGPA:3.20/4.00(80%) 2022-2026
FAST National University of Computer and Emerging Sciences
F.Sc.(Pre-Engineering)-960/1100(87.27%) 2019-2021
Govt. Boys Degree College, Chowk Azam, Layyah, BISE D.G. Khan
Matriculation (Science)-1062/1100 (96.55%) 2017-2019
BISE D.G. Khan
Skills & Interests
ProgrammingLanguages: Python, C++, Java
Web Development : HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Flask, Spring, WordPress
Databases : SQL, MongoDB, SQLAlchemy
Tools &Technologies: Git, n8n, react.js, next.js, docker, Canva, Linux, Oracle, PyTorch, Figma
Research Work
Research Assistant(RA)–Junior Software Engineer
FAST NUCESAugust 2025 – December 2025
4-month paid research on ML-driven gravitational wave analytics; built data pipelines for LIGO/Virgo
datasets and integrated models using Python, TensorFlow, and PyTorch with a multidisciplinary team.
EXPERIENCE
AI Internee July 2025 – August 2025
Shopilam (NICF,UniversityofAgriculture,Faisalabad)
Onsite internship under the supervision of the founder and IT team, building Whatsopify - a WhatsApp
chrome extension. I worked on backend (Python FastAPI) and a Chatbot specially OCR features.
TeachingAssistant
FAST NUCES
1. Information Security &Business Math-II Spring 2026
2. Theory of Automata &Numerical Computing Fall-2025
3. Technical and Business Writing &Linear Algebra Spring-2025
4. Calculus and Analytical Geometry Fall-2023 & 2024
Delivered lectures and managed assessments for large student cohorts, driving improved academic outcomes
and building strong leadership and communication skills.
LEADERSHIP & ACTIVITIES
President-AMNESTY International FAST Chapter June 2025 – Present
·Led a 20-member team, organizing seminars and campaigns to drive human rights awareness.
·Built public speaking, team management, & leadership through hands-on initiative ownership.
Team Leader atJob Fair -entracloud April 2024 & April 2025
·Led entracloud team at FAST Islamabad and CFD Job Fairs to interview final-year students.
·Mentored 25-30 interns in AI, Data Structures, and Databases as a supervisor at entracloud .
PROJECTS :
·AI-powered hiring system with automated job posting, CV parsing, and candidate scoring.
·Includes online test with webcam head and eye detection and job training post-hiring.
·Built a clothing store ( paidproject ) with catalog, cart, checkout, and order tracking.
·Integrated payment, inventory, and responsive design for better performance and SEO.
·Built a Transformer-based Urdu chatbot with preprocessing and modeling for low-resource NLP.
·Trained in PyTorch and deployed with RTL support using Gradio on Hugging Face Spaces.
·A RAG-based diagnostic system on clinical notes using the MIMIC-IV-Ext-Direct dataset.
·Integrated FAISS-powered search with a Flan-T5 model and deployed via Gradio interface.
·An AI-powered physical privacy protection system using OpenCV, YOLO, and MediaPipe.
·Detecting shoulder surfing and unauthorized screen viewing in real time.
·A Seq2Seq image captioning system using ResNet50 feature extraction.
·I used LSTM decoder trained on Flickr30k dataset, achieving BLEU-4 of 0.64.
·Developed a task management app with Flask, SQLAlchemy (CRUD), and a responsive UI
·Developed an IS project using GPT-2 to classify URLs as benign, defacement, or phishing.
·Fine-tuned model with custom tokenization and tokens for accurate URL classification.
·Designed a user-friendly food ordering app prototype in Figma with a clean layout.
·Developed flour mill management system with Java framework, Spring Boot.
CERTIFICATION & AWARDS
·Python and Flask - Udemy ·Git and GitHub - Google
·AI in the classroom – Canva ·WordPress – Digiskills
·Machine Learning Foundations - AWS ·Deep Learning Specialization – Udemy
1st Position winner in the Idea Competition at FAST-NUCES for developing NeuroSecureNeuroHire –Final Year Project
MERN (Next.js and Python (AI))August 2025 – Present
Mashoor Fabrics -E-Commerce ClothingStore
WordPress,WooCommerceLive Ecommerce Store
Urdu Conversational Chatbot–Multi Head Attention Medium Blog
RAG for diagnostic reasoningon clinical notes Colab Code File
NeuroSecure -Secure Screen Monitoring| OpenCV,YOLO,MediaPipe Deployed Link
ViewToVerse -Image Captioning| PyTorch,ResNet50,LSTM Hugging Face Model
To-DoApplication -Python (Flask &SqlAlchemy) GitHub-Repo
PhishingHunter –AI and Security GitHub-Repo
Food PointFront-End –Figma Figma-File
Flour IndustrySystem-SpringBoot-Java GitHub-Repo

roast: This CV reads like it was assembled during a calendar reminder called 'panic'. The content is there, but the impact needs sharper numbers, cleaner structure, and fewer vague claims. Right now the strongest line might be: 'Muhammad Bilal methebilalashiq@gmail.com | +923088660209 | bilal-ashiq | thecallmeBilalAshiq | Faisalabad OBJECTIVE: Final-year Computer Science student at FAST...' and even that needs a gym membership.
serious_feedback: Add measurable outcomes to your experience bullets.
Put your strongest skills and projects near the top.
Replace generic responsibilities with concrete achievements.

summary: Make the summary specific to the role you want.
experience: Use action verbs and numbers for each major bullet.
skills: Group skills by category and remove anything outdated.
projects: Explain the problem, tools used, and result.
education: Keep this concise unless you are early career.

cv_text:
Forename SURNAME 
e-mail: professional email address tel: UK landline or mobile 
Education and Qualifications 
2000-2003 University/Universities Degree and Subject 
 Location; City and Country applicable additional info
 
Work Experience 
 
Sep-07 – Aug-10 Official Company Name City, Country 
Job title 
 Please use 3 -4 bullets maximum to describe your job function & 
responsibilities 
 Concentrate on your achievements, and what you have distinctly 
contributed to in each role, using quantitative examples where possible 
 Examples that may assist you – 
 “Advised client’s Digital Media division on £3M international expansion , 
coordinating a team of 8 analysts during initial research phase ” 
 “Structured and negotiated equip ment deal financing including credit 
purchases, rentals, and 31 lease contracts worth $745k ” 
 
Jun-05 – Sep-07 Official Company Name City, Country 
Job title 
 Make sure your work experience comes to life, consider what someone 
reading your CV would be most interested in 
 Avoid any negativity or short comings on your CV that may raise the 
wrong questions 
 Try to avoid having your CV read like a job description 
 
Mar-04 – Jun-05 Official Company Name City, Country 
Job title 
 Try to ensure your CV is easy to scan, start bullet points with relevant 
action verbs 
 You can also include significant relevant voluntary experience in your 
work experience if it is applicable 
 Try to avoid industry jargon that may not be understood 
 
Aug-03 – Mar-04 Official Company Name City, Country 
Job title 
 Use past tense for roles you have completed 
 Please set dates using the abbreviated month and two digits for the year, 
you must include months as well as years 
 Make sure your CV is an accurate reflection of you and what you want to 
highlight about your experience 
 Stick to facts you can easily discuss. Avoid subjective comments 
 
Additional Information 
 
Interests: Concentrate on activities you participate in and are willing to talk about. You 
should highlight achievements in those activities. Eg. rather than just listing 
‘running’ say ‘running – participa ted in several marathons, P resident of the 
Oxford Runners Club ’ 
 
Achievements: List academic or other achievements here, for example 
 First Class Honours, Previous University 
 Study abroad scholarship (selected 3 out of 600 students) 
 Principal Cellist of London Youth Orchestra 
 
Nationality: your nationality, dual nationality, and any additional work authorization if 
applicable 
Languages: languages other than English and ability level eg. German (fluent)
